{"step_by_step_thinking": "The working time of an impression material refers to the period during which the material is manipulable and can be accurately placed in the oral cavity before it begins to set. For alginate, as described in the documents, the working time begins after mixing and continues until the material starts to exhibit elastic properties. The key point from the documents is that the working time is defined as the period from the start of mixing until just before the material starts to show elastic properties. This is explicitly mentioned in Document [5], which states that the optimum working time for manipulating alginate occurs after the retardation reaction and before the gelation reaction, when the material has not yet started to become elastic. The material must be handled during this window to ensure proper impression formation. Option C directly matches this definition. Option A and B refer to times after or just after elastic properties appear, which is not the working time. Option D, loss of lusture, is not a standard or accurate descriptor of working time. Therefore, C is the most accurate choice.", "answer_choice": "C"}
